key_quote: Certificate coverage at 12.7% (95/750 test-model pairs) is universally noted as too low — the vast majority of pairs lack machine-checked proofs, and the selection criteria are undocumented.
rationale: All five reviewers (mean 7.0, range 6-8) recognize that the predict-then-verify architecture occupies a useful niche. Chang scores 8/10, praising sound Z3 formalization and genuine litmus synthesis. If the certificate coverage were expanded, DSL-to-.cat correspondence proved, and compositional reasoning addressed, reviewers would not say 'not novel.' The primary blockers are low certificate coverage (12.7%), no DSL-to-.cat formal correspondence, no compositional reasoning, and shallow SMT usage — all depth/rigor/completeness issues. Category B.
